subject: [PATCH] nfsd: avoid leaking pre-allocated openowner on unconfirmed retry race

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Identified the patch as a valid fix for the memory leak issue and applied it to the nfsd-testing branch without requesting any changes.
reviews.raw_body:
From: Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

On Fri, 22 May 2026 10:36:14 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> When find_or_alloc_open_stateowner() encounters an unconfirmed owner, it
> calls release_openowner() and sets oo = NULL. Control then falls through
> past the `if (oo)` guard \u2014 which would have freed any pre-allocated
> `new` \u2014 and unconditionally executes `new = alloc_stateowner(...)`. If
> `new` was already allocated on a prior iteration, the pointer is
> silently overwritten and the previous allocation (slab object + owner
> name buffer) is leaked.
> 
> [...]

Applied to nfsd-testing, thanks!

[1/1] nfsd: avoid leaking pre-allocated openowner on unconfirmed retry race
      commit: f36ecdd78c6271239579ad7fb3d0a51697160877

--
Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

2026-05-22.patch_summary: The patch addresses a potential memory leak in the NFSv4.0 OPEN thread by adding a goto statement to reuse an already-allocated openowner on subsequent iterations, preventing overwriting and leakage of previously allocated slab objects and owner name buffers.
